---
title: "Warty Sedge (Carex verrucosa)"
slug: "carex-verrucosa"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/621932029/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Warty Sedge"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Liliopsida"
tax_order: "Poales"
tax_family: "Cyperaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Liliopsida", "Poales", "Cyperaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/621932029/medium.jpg" alt="Warty Sedge" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/swagner23">
swagner23</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/341613803">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Liliopsida
-   **Order:** Poales
-   **Family:** Cyperaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=160139)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Uncommon species in Florida found in wet pinelands and depression marshes.

## Natural history notes

A grass-like plant characterized by its narrow leaves and distinctive seed structures with a rough, warty texture. In Florida, it is found in moist to wet habitats such as marshes, wet flatwoods, and along pond margins. It grows during the warmer months and contributes to plant diversity and structure in wetland ecosystems.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/160139-Carex-verrucosa)
-   [Local profile](https://explorer.natureserve.org/Taxon/ELEMENT_GLOBAL.2.155030/Carex_verrucosa)
